title: 字母袖扣谋杀案
summary:
Samuel Kid meets with Poirot to provide information regarding the murders at the Blaukenshahn Hotel. Kid reveals that he recognized the woman who fled the hotel after dropping two keys; having seen her picture in a newspaper, he identifies her as the well-known portrait painter Nancy Drew. Kid's face is covered in injuries, which he explains were caused by his own shaking hands while using a razor.

The following day, Mr. Kitcher visits Margaret Ernst at her cottage near St. Gregory's churchyard. Kitcher presents Margaret with a list of guests and staff from the Blaukenshahn Hotel. Margaret agrees with Kid's identification, asserting that Nancy Drew is the only person with the motive, ability, and means to have killed Harriet Sipper, Ada Gransbury, and Richard Nigges. While Margaret finds it difficult to believe the well-mannered Nancy could commit such crimes, she notes that Nancy lived in Gleyholling until 1913, the same year Patric and Frances Ives died and Richard Nigges left the village. Margaret dismisses Walter Sturridge as a suspect due to his physical infirmity, despite his connection to the deceased Frances Ives.

Kitcher requests that Margaret recount the story of Patric and Frances Ives to help resolve the mysteries. Margaret agrees to tell the story on the condition that Kitcher swears not to repeat any of it to anyone in the village until he leaves for London. She warns him that after hearing her account, he will likely want to find the doctor, Ambrose Flavard.
